TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}

S.FORM: ka sitsuayan izua za Taruvar i pairang; avan azua kakalipatan nua zaɫum.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan izua za taruvar i pairaŋ; avan azua kakaɭipatan nua zaʎum.
S.FORM: ka sicuayan izua za Taruvar i pairang; avan azua kakalipatan nua zaljum.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan izua za taruvar i pairaŋ; avan azua kakaɭipatan nua zaʎum.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time there was a monster Taruvar on the plains, and all the water went in there.

S.FORM: manu maɫupetj azua angaɫ ni Taruvar.
S.PHON: manu maʎupəc azua aŋaʎ ni taruvar.
S.FORM: manu maljupetj azua angalj ni Taruvar.
S.PHON: manu maʎupəʦ azua aŋaʎ ni taruvar.

S.FORM: matevuk a zaɫum; meqatsa azua zaɫum.
S.PHON: matəvuk a zaʎum; məqaʦa azua zaʎum.
S.FORM: matevuk a zaljum; meqaca azua zaljum.
S.PHON: matəvuk a zaʎum; məqaʦa azua zaʎum.
S.TRANSL: The water accumulated; and the water level rose.

S.FORM: azua keḍikeḍian a gade malimu tua zaɫum tua kinaqatsa.
S.PHON: azua kəɖikəɖian a gadə maɭimu tua zaʎum tua kinaqaʦa.
S.FORM: azua kedrikedrian a gade malimu tua zaljum tua kinaqaca.
S.PHON: azua kəɖikəɖian a gadə maɭimu tua zaʎum tua kinaqaʦa.
S.TRANSL: The smaller mountains were all covered because of the great volume of water.

S.FORM: amin anga i Tumapalapalai kata i Palasidang kata i Kavulungan inika namalimu ta zaɫum.
S.PHON: amin aŋa i tumapaɭapaɭai kata i paɭasidaŋ kata i kavuɭuŋan inika namaɭimu ta zaʎum.
S.FORM: amin anga i Tumapalapalai kata i Palasidang kata i Kavulungan inika namalimu ta zaljum.
S.PHON: amin aŋa i tumapaɭapaɭai kata i paɭasidaŋ kata i kavuɭuŋan inika namaɭimu ta zaʎum.
S.TRANSL: Only Tumapalapalai, Palasidang and Kavulungan mountains were not covered by the water.

S.FORM: azua tsautsau a se Kapayuanan sema zua i Tumapalapalai.
S.PHON: azua ʦauʦau a sə kapajuanan səma zua i tumapaɭapaɭai.
S.FORM: azua caucau a se Kapayuanan sema zua i Tumapalapalai.
S.PHON: azua ʦauʦau a sə kapajuanan səma zua i tumapaɭapaɭai.
S.TRANSL: The people of Kapayuanan went to Tumapalapalai.

S.FORM: izua neka nu sapuy.
S.PHON: izua nəka nu sapuj.
S.FORM: izua neka nu sapuy.
S.PHON: izua nəka nu sapuj.

S.FORM: izua za takets; sekauɫan nua tsautsau pasa zua kisapuy tua Palasidang azua takets, a sema zua i Palasidang a ɫemanguy uta pasa maza; mangtjez i Tumapalapalai.
S.PHON: izua za takəʦ; səkauʎan nua ʦauʦau pasa zua kisapuj tua paɭasidaŋ azua takəʦ, a səma zua i paɭasidaŋ a ʎəmaŋuj uta pasa maza; maŋcəz i tumapaɭapaɭai.
S.FORM: izua za takec; sekauljan nua caucau pasa zua kisapuy tua Palasidang azua takec, a sema zua i Palasidang a ljemanguy uta pasa maza; mangtjez i Tumapalapalai.
S.PHON: izua za takəʦ; səkauʎan nua ʦauʦau pasa zua kisapuj tua paɭasidaŋ azua takəʦ, a səma zua i paɭasidaŋ a ʎəmaŋuj uta pasa maza; maŋʦəz i tumapaɭapaɭai.
S.TRANSL: There was a muntjac, and the people sent it to Palasidang to get some fire. The muntjac swam there to Palasidang andcame back here to Tumapalapalai.

S.FORM: kemsanga azua (i) Tumapalapalai.
S.PHON: kəmsaŋa azua (i) tumapaɭapaɭai.
S.FORM: kemsanga azua (i) Tumapalapalai.
S.PHON: kəmsaŋa azua (i) tumapaɭapaɭai.
S.TRANSL: And so they could cook on Tumapalapalai.

S.FORM: ka tsuatsuay anga masuɫupetj azua angaɫ ni Taruvar; masutevuk anga; mekeḍi anga a zaɫum.
S.PHON: ka ʦuaʦuaj aŋa masuʎupəc azua aŋaʎ ni taruvar; masutəvuk aŋa; məkəɖi aŋa a zaʎum.
S.FORM: ka cuacuay anga masuljupetj azua angalj ni Taruvar; masutevuk anga; mekedri anga a zaljum.
S.PHON: ka ʦuaʦuaj aŋa masuʎupəʦ azua aŋaʎ ni taruvar; masutəvuk aŋa; məkəɖi aŋa a zaʎum.
S.TRANSL: After a long time the mouth of Taruvar was unblocked, the water drained away, and the water level went down.

S.FORM: izua za qatjulaɫ masekelay tua kasiw; djumaken nua mamazangiɫan.
S.PHON: izua za qacuɭaʎ masəkəɭaj tua kasiw; ɉumakən nua mamazaŋiʎan.
S.FORM: izua za qatjulalj masekelay tua kasiw; djumaken nua mamazangiljan.
S.PHON: izua za qaʦuɭaʎ masəkəɭaj tua kasiw; ɉumakən nua mamazaŋiʎan.
S.TRANSL: There were worms hanging on the trees. The chiefs found them

S.FORM: paquzipen tua lami nua mamazangiɫan.
S.PHON: paquzipən tua ɭami nua mamazaŋiʎan.
S.FORM: paquzipen tua lami nua mamazangiljan.
S.PHON: paquzipən tua ɭami nua mamazaŋiʎan.

S.FORM: azua qatjulaɫ nu tjeqatsang, masan qipu.
S.PHON: azua qacuɭaʎ nu cəqaʦaŋ, masan qipu.
S.FORM: azua qatjulalj nu tjeqacang, masan qipu.
S.PHON: azua qaʦuɭaʎ nu ʦəqaʦaŋ, masan qipu.
S.TRANSL: When the worms defecated, it became soil.

S.FORM: avan anga sika izua nu sa qipu.
S.PHON: avan aŋa sika izua nu sa qipu.
S.FORM: avan anga sika izua nu sa qipu.
S.PHON: avan aŋa sika izua nu sa qipu.
S.TRANSL: That’s the reason there is soil;

S.FORM: saka aitsu a kadjunangan azua sika nu mamazangiɫan, ayatua tiamadju a napaquzip ta qatjulaɫ.
S.PHON: saka aiʦu a kaɉunaŋan azua sika nu mamazaŋiʎan, ajatua tiamaɉu a napaquzip ta qacuɭaʎ.
S.FORM: saka aicu a kadjunangan azua sika nu mamazangiljan, ayatua tiamadju a napaquzip ta qatjulalj.
S.PHON: saka aiʦu a kaɉunaŋan azua sika nu mamazaŋiʎan, ajatua tiamaɉu a napaquzip ta qaʦuɭaʎ.
S.TRANSL: and that’s the reason why all the land belongs to the chiefs, because they fed the worms.
